---
title: "Hands-On Demo: Sharing and Publishing"
subtitle: "Publishing reproducible reports with GitLab Pages and Zenodo"
author:
  - name: "Gorka Fraga González"
    orcid: 0000-0002-1857-8607
  - name: "Fabio Molo"
    orcid: 0000-0001-9320-5854
format:
  html:
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  eval: false
---

This hands-on demo requires Git and a GitLab (or GitHub) account. Visit the [instructions](../../instructions/index.qmd) page for setup details.

# Publishing with GitLab Pages

<!-- TODO: add introductory exercises here -->

## Exercise 1: Setting up a Quarto website project

<!-- TODO -->

## Exercise 2: Configuring a CI/CD pipeline for GitLab Pages

<!-- TODO -->

## Exercise 3: Making your site public

<!-- TODO -->

# Persistent identifiers and archiving

## Exercise 4: Creating a GitLab release

<!-- TODO -->

## Exercise 5: Archiving to Zenodo and obtaining a DOI

<!-- TODO -->

# Preregistration

## Exercise 6: Setting up a preregistration on OSF

<!-- TODO -->

# Additional Resources

-   Quarto websites documentation: <https://quarto.org/docs/websites/>
-   GitLab Pages documentation: <https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/pages/>
-   Zenodo: <https://zenodo.org>
-   Open Science Framework (OSF): <https://osf.io>
